name: IMCPClient
description: Port interface for Model Context Protocol (MCP) server clients. Implementations bridge the tool primitive to external MCP servers, enabling access to the MCP tool ecosystem without custom adapters. Transport-agnostic — concrete clients handle stdio, SSE, or HTTP communication.

methods.name: connect
methods.description: Establish connection to an MCP server.

methods.name: list_tools
methods.description: Discover available tools on the connected server. Returns a list of tool descriptors with name, description, and input schema for each available tool.

methods.name: call_tool

params.name: name
params.description: Tool name as returned by list_tools()

params.name: arguments
params.description: Tool arguments matching the tool's input schema

methods.description: Invoke a tool on the connected server. Returns the tool execution result.

methods.name: disconnect
methods.description: Close the connection to the MCP server.

behavioral_expectations: Must implement structural subtyping (Protocol) — no explicit inheritance required
connect() must support multiple transport schemes: stdio://, sse://, http://
list_tools() must only be called after a successful connect(); behavior is undefined otherwise
call_tool() must validate that the tool name exists on the connected server
disconnect() must not raise if not currently connected (idempotent close)
All methods are async — MCP communication involves network I/O
Tool descriptors returned by list_tools() must include at minimum: name, description, and input_schema fields
Implementations must handle connection failures and timeouts gracefully
